desc: Munnar is Kerala's flagship hill station, with cool tea-country weather, foggy monsoon months, and clear highland views in the drier season.
hls: Lush monsoon scenery with lower prices
Great base for 2–3 day nature breaks

todo.name: Tea museum + estate visit
todo.desc: Learn tea history and walk viewpoints through active plantations.

todo.name: Eravikulam National Park
todo.desc: Book timed entry for Nilgiri tahr sightings and rolling highland views.

todo.name: Top Station sunrise
todo.desc: Start early for valley cloudscapes before day-trip traffic builds.

todo.name: Mattupetty–Echo Point circuit
todo.desc: Pair reservoir viewpoints with short forest-edge walks.

todo.name: Tea-town café afternoon
todo.desc: Use cool evenings for relaxed café and bakery hopping.

todo.name: Anamudi foothill viewpoints
todo.desc: Choose short highland trails in drier months for clearer visibility.

wifi.note: Usable in town hotels; weaker in estate stays and during heavy rain.

prac.fltNote: Nearest major airport is Kochi (COK). Munnar sits in the hills about 125 km away; expect 4–5 hours by road (often longer on weekends/holidays due to mountain traffic and weather).
prac.lang: Malayalam, Tamil, English common in tourism
prac.visa: eVisa available for many nationalities; verify current category rules

sweet: December to March is coolest and clearest for tea-country viewpoints, with heavy rains in monsoon months.
